description: Foundational tracks 1-5 for DIY Language Transfer Mandarin Chinese.

tracks.title: Tones & SVO Structure
tracks.newConcept: Mandarin Tones & Basic SVO Word Order
tracks.conceptNote: Tones are voice contours. 1st: high/flat; 2nd: rising; 3rd: dipping; 4th: falling. Basic word order is Subject-Verb-Object.

steps.text: Welcome to Language Transfer Mandarin Chinese! In this course, we will build Mandarin from the ground up using logic and transfer. Let's start with tones: think of them not as musical notes, but as natural English intonations. The 4th tone is like an emphatic 'No!'. The 2nd tone is like asking 'Huh?'.

steps.prompt: How would you say: 'I drink tea'?
steps.hint: Mandarin word order is identical to English Subject-Verb-Object.
steps.followUp: Notice how simple that is! No verb conjugations whatsoever.

tracks.title: Questions with Ma
tracks.conceptNote: Add 吗 to the end of any statement to turn it into a yes/no question.

steps.text: To turn any statement into a yes/no question in Mandarin, you don't invert word order like in English. You simply add the spoken question mark particle 吗 (ma) to the very end of the sentence.

steps.prompt: How would you ask: 'Do you drink tea?'
steps.hint: Start with 'You drink tea' and add the question particle at the end.

tracks.title: Negation with Bu
tracks.conceptNote: Place 不 directly before the verb to negate present or future actions.

steps.text: To say you do not do something, place 不 (bù) immediately before the verb. Notice our first phonetic loanword: kāfēi (coffee)!

steps.prompt: How would you say: 'I don't drink coffee'?
steps.hint: Place 不 before the verb 'to drink'.
steps.followUp: Spot on. Wǒ bù hē kāfēi.

tracks.title: Wanting with Yao
tracks.conceptNote: 要 precedes the noun or action verb to express desire or requirement.

steps.text: The verb 要 (yào) means 'to want'. Just like in English, you can place it before a noun ('want tea') or before another verb ('want to drink').

steps.prompt: How would you ask: 'Do you want to drink coffee?'
steps.hint: Combine 'You want drink coffee' with the question particle ma.
steps.followUp: Great job building multi-verb sentences effortlessly.

tracks.title: Possession with De
tracks.conceptNote: Attach 的 to a pronoun or noun to indicate possession (like English 's).

steps.text: To say 'my' or 'his', attach the connecting particle 的 (de) after the pronoun. So wǒ is 'I', and wǒ de is 'my'.

steps.prompt: How would you say: 'He wants my tea'?
steps.hint: Use tā for 'he' and wǒ de for 'my'.
steps.followUp: Awesome. You now have the core building blocks of Mandarin!
